channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏴 Ukrainian long-range drones are turning Russia’s size into a weakness | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukraine is waging a strategic bombing campaign of deep strikes across the Russian Federation that aims to exploit Russia's colossal size and transform it from a key strength into a fatal weakness, writes Peter Dickinson. The post Ukrainian long-range drones are turning Russia’s…

item.title: 🏛️ Hegseth rethinking Army reforms, cuts to aviation | Defense One
item.description: One year after launching the Army Transformation Initiative, SecDef says he's reviewing the to-do list.

item.title: 🕵️ US, close allies creating joint ‘orbital warfare’ plan: SPACECOM chief | Breaking Defense
item.description: Gen. Stephen Whiting told the Mitchell Institute today that SPACECOM also plans to Apollo Insights table-top exercises (TTXs) with commercial industry once a quarter this year.

item.title: 🏛️ Hegseth: Pentagon is ‘taking another look’ at deep cuts to Army’s aircraft budget | Breaking Defense
item.description: Lawmakers expressed concern over the Army’s proposed budget, which takes an ax to high-profile legacy helicopter procurement.

item.title: 🏛️ Defense contractors have donated millions to members of Congress, new report finds | Defense News
item.description: The defense industry donations make up only a small percentage of the cash going to Congress, however, according to OpenSecrets.

item.title: 🏴 EU targets Kyrgyzstan as Brussels seeks to prevent Russian sanctions evasion | Atlantic Council
item.description: The latest EU sanctions on Russia also featured measures against Kyrgyzstan. This was the first use of so-called anti-circumvention tools, which are designed to prevent third countries from helping the Kremlin bypass restrictions over the invasion of Ukraine, writes Marc…

item.title: 🏴 Drone-Hunting M28 Skytruck In The Works For Poland | The War Zone
item.description: With the Ukrainian An-28 reportedly highly effective in the role, Poland is converting its similar M28 for the counter-drone mission. The post Drone-Hunting M28 Skytruck In The Works For Poland appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 The missing link in the US energy advantage: Connecting supply to demand | Atlantic Council
item.description: The US has the energy supplies to help meet rising demand—its challenge is building the infrastructure to connect one to the other. Permitting reform could change this. The post The missing link in the US energy advantage: Connecting supply to demand appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🏴 Something else for Australia to consider: Russian nuclear subs in the Pacific | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Strategists in Canberra should factor in the plausible risk of Russian participation in a Western Pacific conflict, especially action by Russian submarines. Russia has modernised its Pacific submarines and has a clear reason for using ...

item.title: 🏛️ IRGC Navy Claims Vast Expansion In Its Definition Of Strait Of Hormuz (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: It remains unclear how the IRGC will enforce its tenfold increase in what it considers the Strait. The post IRGC Navy Claims Vast Expansion In Its Definition Of Strait Of Hormuz (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Does OPEC Still Matter? | War on the Rocks
item.description: On April 28, the United Arab Emirates announced that it would leave OPEC, effective May 1 — ending nearly six decades as an OPEC member. In terms of oil production, it is the most significant country to leave the group. While multiple factors drove the decision to leave, the…

item.title: 🌏 China’s Rare-Earth Card Looms Over Trump-Xi Summit | Foreign Policy
item.description: For all of the U.S. leader’s efforts, Washington remains deeply vulnerable.

item.title: 📍 Chinese Hegemony Might Be Happening | Foreign Policy
item.description: There were always reasons to doubt it was a real possibility—until now.

item.title: 📍 What’s Driving the K-Shaped Economy? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Uneven wealth distribution in the United States has political—and global—implications.

item.title: 📍 Asia Isn’t as Peaceful as It Seems | Foreign Policy
item.description: Trouble is brewing as norms against war dissolve.

item.title: 🌍 Price tag for Iran war ticks up to $29B, not including base damage | Breaking Defense
item.description: “We’re not making an estimate for MILCON [military construction] at this time. We don’t know what our future posture is going to be,” said Pentagon comptroller Jules Hurst.

item.title: 🌍 Would We Be Better Off Today With the JCPOA? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Iran war spotlights one of Washington’s most contentious counterfactuals.

item.title: 🌍 Congress waits on Iran-war costs while mulling $1.5T defense request | Defense One
item.description: The administration’s latest estimate is $29B, but the Defense Department hasn’t answered lawmakers’ requests for details.

item.title: 🏛️ Golden Dome-style missile shield could cost up to $1.2T over 20 years, CBO estimates | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Congressional Budget Office said it was “impossible” to get a true cost for Golden Dome, as DoD hasn’t revealed details about its envisioned architecture.

item.title: 🕵️ ‘It would be insane’ for spy agencies to lack early access to AI models, lawmaker says | Defense One
item.description: Commerce Department should also have a role in AI policy, top Democrat on the House Intelligence Committee says.

item.title: 🏛️ Golden Dome plan would cost $1.2 trillion, CBO finds | Defense One
item.description: That’s seven times what Trump initially said, and almost double the congressional office’s first estimate.

item.title: 📍 What Does SOUTHCOM’s New Autonomous Warfare Command Herald? | War on the Rocks
item.description: Gen. Frank Donovan, commander of U.S. Southern Command, sat down with Ryan to discuss the vision behind the command’s new Autonomous Warfare Command and what it signals for the future of military operations in the Western Hemisphere. Donovan explains how SOUTHCOM aims to move…

item.title: ⚛️ New Version Of Bomber-Launched ARRW Hypersonic Missile Is A Ship Killer | The War Zone
item.description: The USAF is moving to start work on a new ARRW variant able to hit moving targets with an eye toward future fights in the Pacific. The post New Version Of Bomber-Launched ARRW Hypersonic Missile Is A Ship Killer appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Why the U.S. Is Headed for a Long War With Iran | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Tehran regime is more hard-line than ever—and has nothing left to lose.

item.title: 🌏 Uncovering China’s attacks on investigative journalism in the Philippines | Atlantic Council
item.description: In recent months, the Chinese Embassy in Manila has issued several statements attacking the Philippine Center for Investigative Journalism. The post Uncovering China’s attacks on investigative journalism in the Philippines appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Australia unveils $45.2 billion defense budget with ramp up in AUKUS spending | Breaking Defense
item.description: This year’s budget represents a six percent increase over last year’s defense spending.

item.title: 🌏 A 7-Year-Old American Boy Is Trapped in China Because His Father Made Art | The Diplomat
item.description: In what can only be described as collective punishment, the young son of detained artist Gao Zhen is being prevented from returning home to the United States

item.title: 🌏 Quick Take: The Leader Gets a Strong Constitution | 38 North
item.description: The most recent North Korean (Democratic People’s Republic of Korea or DPRK) constitution was ... The post Quick Take: The Leader Gets a Strong Constitution appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: 🌍 Pentagon seeks additional funding as cost of Iran war tops $29 billion | Defense News
item.description: The increase from $25 billion just two weeks ago reflects “updated repair and replacement of equipment costs,” along with the “general operational costs."

item.title: 🏛️ Hegseth says E-7 Wedgetail ‘has a future,’ reversing planned cancellation | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Boeing E-7 Wedgetail previously faced the axe under the Trump administration, but pressure from lawmakers and a new “mindset” at the Pentagon have revived its prospects.
